name: Merkle Tree
def: A tree of hashes where each node hashes its children, summarising a dataset in one root hash.
problem: Verify integrity and detect differences in large datasets with compact, logarithmic proofs.
mechanism: Leaves hash data blocks; internal nodes hash concatenated child hashes up to a single root. A membership proof is the sibling hashes along one path.
benefits: Tamper-evident: any change alters the root
limits: Proof verification needs the trusted root
failures: Second-preimage attacks without leaf/node domain separation
Trusting a root from an untrusted source
whenNot: Small data where a single hash suffices
myths: "Merkle trees are only for blockchains" — Git, ZFS, Cassandra and BitTorrent use them too
